summary: Excellent audience recording captures a tight, soulful show with soaring Jerry solos and standout jams on Eyes of the World and It Must Have Been The Roses.
review: The audience tape is praised as crystal‑clear and one of the best available for this concert. Musically the band delivers a solid performance – the first set warms up around Althea, then erupts into sublime jamming on Eyes of the World, while the second set stays tight with a monster It Must Have Been The Roses and memorable solos on They Love Each Other and El Paso.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers gave 4‑5 stars, highlighting an excellent recording and a strong performance with several standout moments; the consistency of praise yields a high confidence rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as ‘excellent audience recording’, ‘crisp’, and “best of all uploaded versions”, with no noted dropouts or major flaws.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been the Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts rough but gains momentum around Althea; second set stays tight with strong transitions (e.g., Shakedown → Minglewood) and a particularly tight, jam‑light performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note soaring solos on El Paso, They Love Each Other, and overall tasteful guitar work.
